title: The Journey from Population to Sampling Distribution
description: A flowchart illustrating the process of how repeated sampling from a population leads to the formation of a sampling distribution of a statistic (e.g., the sample mean).
elements: Calculated Sample Statistic (e.g., x̄) for each sample
Sampling Distribution (of the statistic, e.g., x̄)
relationships: Population is the source for drawing Repeated Random Samples
Each Random Sample is used to calculate a Sample Statistic
All calculated Sample Statistics are collected
The distribution of these collected statistics forms the Sampling Distribution
